meaning: A short text message sent and received in real time over the internet
example: She sent an instant message to ask if he was free to talk.

meaning: A private message sent to a specific individual on a platform
example: Send me a direct message if you have any questions.

meaning: A messaging conversation involving three or more participants
example: The project team uses a group chat for quick daily updates.

meaning: A small digital image or icon used to express emotion in text
example: She added a thumbs-up emoji to show her agreement.

meaning: A short, looping animated image used in digital communication
example: He replied to the good news with a celebratory GIF.

meaning: A larger, illustrated digital image used like an emoji in messaging apps
example: The app offers custom sticker packs for expressive chats.

meaning: A notification indicating that the recipient has seen a message
example: The blue ticks serve as read receipts in many messaging apps.

meaning: An alert informing a user of a new message or event
example: He turned off notifications during the meeting to avoid distractions.

meaning: To highlight and fix an important message at the top of a chat
example: The manager pinned the meeting agenda in the team chat.

meaning: Tagging a specific person in a message using @ to alert them
example: Use @John in the chat to make sure he sees the update.

meaning: An emoji-based response to a specific message without typing a reply
example: He gave a heart reaction to show appreciation for the update.

meaning: A series of replies nested under a specific message in a chat
example: Reply in the thread to keep the main channel uncluttered.

meaning: A status setting that suppresses incoming message notifications
example: She activated do not disturb during focused work hours.

meaning: An automated program that simulates conversation with users
example: The chatbot answered basic customer queries instantly.

meaning: A message that is encoded so only the intended recipient can read it
example: The app sends encrypted messages to protect user privacy.

meaning: To send a brief message to get someone's attention quickly
example: Ping me on Slack when the document is ready to review.

meaning: Choosing to receive messages or communications voluntarily
example: Users must opt-in to receive marketing emails.

meaning: A message sent to many recipients at once, typically one-way
example: The company sent a broadcast message to announce the new policy.

meaning: A dedicated space in a messaging platform for a specific topic or team
example: Post design-related updates in the #design channel.

meaning: An image representing a user on a digital platform or messaging app
example: Use a professional profile picture for work-related platforms.

meaning: A username or identifier used on a digital platform
example: Her Twitter handle is @sarahdesigns.

meaning: Linking a person or topic in a post or message to draw attention
example: She posted the update and tagged the relevant team members.

meaning: A status message informing others that a user is temporarily unavailable
example: His away message said he would be back in an hour.

meaning: A security method ensuring only communicating users can read the messages
example: The app uses end-to-end encryption to protect private conversations.
